action.hypothesis: `lowrank_grouped_residual` clearly lost at `K=16`, so the severe-stagnation policy still points to another fundamentally different architecture rather than more scalar tweaks. `whitened_lowrank_gated_residual` is a materially different low-EF family because it changes the support-selection space through residual preconditioning plus gating, and testing it at the frontier-relevant `K=16, EF=12` slot can reveal whether that combination improves the mid-K transition band over `lowrank_two_stage_residual`.
action.summary: Run a pure architecture comparison at `K=16, EF=12` by switching from `lowrank_two_stage_residual` to `whitened_lowrank_gated_residual` while keeping optimizer, learning rate, expansion factor, and aux fixed to the settled mid-K recipe. This follows severe-stagnation guidance by opening another substantially different built-in architecture branch instead of continuing minor sweeps.
action.self_review: This is the most policy-compliant next step after round 40: stop scalar tweaks and try a genuinely different architecture. `K=16` remains the cleanest comparison point because it is frontier-relevant, controlled, and gives a high-signal readout on whether preconditioned gated support selection helps in the mid-K lane.
notes_to_memory: Round 41 rejects `lowrank_grouped_residual` at `K=16` and continues the severe-stagnation architecture search with another materially different built-in family rather than returning to LR or aux sweeps.

The new test is `whitened_lowrank_gated_residual` at `K=16, EF=12` under the settled `signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` recipe to evaluate whether residual preconditioning plus gating improves the frontier-relevant mid-K band.
next_hypotheses: If `whitened_lowrank_gated_residual` wins at `K=16`, continue the same architecture-only comparison at `K=24` before changing any scalar hyperparameters.
If it is close but slightly worse at `K=16`, keep the family active and test one adjacent K to see whether the benefit is band-specific.
If it clearly loses at `K=16`, reject this branch and move to another substantially different built-in architecture rather than minor sweeps.

result.description: Run a pure architecture comparison at `K=16, EF=12` by switching from `lowrank_two_stage_residual` to `whitened_lowrank_gated_residual` while keeping optimizer, learning rate, expansion factor, and aux fixed to the settled mid-K recipe. This follows severe-stagnation guidance by opening another substantially different built-in architecture branch instead of continuing minor sweeps.
result.self_review: This is the most policy-compliant next step after round 40: stop scalar tweaks and try a genuinely different architecture. `K=16` remains the cleanest comparison point because it is frontier-relevant, controlled, and gives a high-signal readout on whether preconditioned gated support selection helps in the mid-K lane.
